summary: Promote the Surf-backed *http.Client in diary_get_day.go to a package-level sync.Once singleton so multi-date reads reuse TLS connections; extract only Surf's Transport and wrap it in a jar-less *http.Client.
reason: fetchAuthenticatedHTML previously built a fresh Surf client (and its TLS connection pool) on every call, costing a full TLS handshake per date. Reusing the full Surf *http.Client (which carries a CookieJar from Impersonate().Chrome()) would have accumulated Set-Cookie responses from MFP and re-sent them alongside the manually-set Cookie: <session> header on subsequent calls, producing duplicate/conflicting cookies. Mirrors newHTTPClient's pattern in internal/client/client.go.
